title: Tragacantha schrenkiana
intro: Tragacantha schrenkiana, also known as Schrenk's milkvetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha schrenkiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, gastrointestinal disorders, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Tragacantha schrenkiana belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and is further classified within the tribe Hedysareae. The genus Tragacantha is a group of plants that are often associated with the genus Astragalus. However, some sources consider Tragacantha to be a synonym for Astragalus, which would place Tragacantha schrenkiana within the genus Astragalus. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Tragacantha schrenkiana.
